utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good morning rebecca , nice to see you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: nice to see you too .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , my nurse told you a little bit about dax , now i'm wondering if i can tell dax a little bit about you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: go for it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , so rebecca is a 27-year-old female patient . um , you have a history of carpal tunnel , um , uh , release done couple of years ago , you have some chronic back pain , some depression . why am i seeing you today ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so last week , um , and over the course of the month , i have taken up swimming more regularly .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i've been trying to get up and get active . and i've- ... all of a sudden i've been feeling some really bad , um , knee pain-

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: . in my right knee .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: just in your right knee ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . just when swimming ? are you swimming freestyle with your legs kicking straight or more like breaststroke where your legs kick out ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , it's only while swimming. i use the breaststroke so i guess where the legs are kicking out like a frog ... it kinda starts hurting a little bit after i swim a couple laps , which a month ago when i started i was able to do ten .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: but after a couple of laps it starts to hurt .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and no injury ? like you did n't fall or twist it or anything like that that you remember ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , not that i can remember .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so just swimming a lot and you feeling more and more discomfort ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , good . so , um ... i know my nurse did a review of systems before you came in . there were really no significant issues . you have a history of chronic back pain , that's been doing well ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , it comes and goes , but right now it's been doing really well .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , no pain going down your legs or anything like that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and i know you have some depression , you've been on medication . how's that been going ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that's been going really well .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: with the medication and therapy and then trying to get outside more it's been really helping me .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: sleeping well ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: eating well ? okay . do n't feel any concerns about hurting yourself or others or anything like that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , good . um , and the , you had ca- carpal tunnel done a few years ago . how's that doing ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that's doing-

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: any flare up with that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: . that's doing really well . i've been getting full range of motion and it's felt a lot better .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , great . do you mind if i do a quick exam ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , go for it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: squeeze my fingers for me . good . okay . is it painful if i move your patella like that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , that hurts .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and i'm gon na hold your leg out , i'm gon na hold , i want you to stiffen your upper ... does that hurt when i do that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , that hurts too .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , good , all right . um , let's take a quick look at your x-ray , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dragon , show me the right knee x-ray . okay , so here's a picture of your right knee . actually normal x-ray , everything looks good , okay ? on my exam , um , you do n't really have any swelling in- in your joints , um , uh , of your knee . you have a little pain with , um , uh , range of motion of the knee . you have real pain when we do what we call patella in- ... patellar inhibition , okay ? so that's what caused most of that pain . so , um , your heart is regular , your lungs are clear , i do n't feel any adenopathy , your thyroid's , um , normal , your grips are equal , the rest of your exam is essentially normal , okay ? so my assessment is , you have what we call chondromalacia of the patella , okay ? it's just from the overuse and all- all the swimming you've been doing , okay ? so , um , to treat that we'll just use some anti-inflammatories , um , go ahead and take some ibuprofen , 600 milligrams four times a day with food . i do want you to do some straight leg raising exercises , that'll strengthen your quadriceps and that'll help with that pain , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , basically you want to avoid anything that puts extra pressure on your knees for a little while and it should be fine . if it's not getting any better in a couple weeks i wan na see you again , okay ? so no lunges , avoid the breaststroke . if you're comfortable, you can also do other swimming strokes where your knees are straight instead . or you can take the opportunity to have a little break .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: all right .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: your second problem is your chronic back pain , that's been doing really well . um , keep swimming , that's okay after this gets better .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , but i think , um , i think that's been under control . but if it flares up go ahead and , uh , give me a call , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , your third problem is your depression . that's been stable . we're gon na keep you on your current medication , not gon na change anything at this point , okay ? and as far as your carpal tunnel goes , that's relatively stable , so , um , uh , no changes there as well . okay ? so anything else i can do for you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , that's it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , well we'll get you checked out .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: all right .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dragon , finalize the report .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 27-year-old female with a history of carpal tunnel release, chronic back pain, and depression.
Joint pain
The patient has increased her swimming activity over the past month.
The patient reports experiencing significant right knee pain.
The doctor asks if the pain is localized only to the right knee.
The doctor asks about the patient's swimming technique and leg movement.
The patient uses the breaststroke and experiences knee pain after a few laps, whereas she could previously swim ten laps.
The doctor asks if there was any specific injury or trauma to the knee.
The patient denies any known injury or trauma to the knee.
Chronic back pain
The doctor asks about the status of the patient's chronic back pain.
The patient reports her chronic back pain is currently doing well.
The doctor asks if the patient has any pain radiating down her legs.
The patient denies leg pain radiation.
Depression
The doctor asks how the patient's depression is being managed.
The patient reports her depression is well-managed with medication, therapy, and increased outdoor activity.
The doctor asks if the patient is sleeping well.
The doctor asks if the patient is eating well and denies suicidal or homicidal ideation.
Carpal tunnel
The patient reports her carpal tunnel is doing well with full range of motion.
The doctor performs a physical exam, including checking grip strength and patellar movement.
The right knee x-ray is normal, and the physical exam shows pain with patellar inhibition and range of motion, but no swelling.
The doctor assesses the patient with chondromalacia of the patella due to overuse.
The doctor prescribes ibuprofen 600 mg four times a day with food.
The doctor instructs the patient to perform straight leg raising exercises to strengthen quadriceps.
The doctor advises avoiding pressure on the knees, specifically breaststroke, and suggests a follow-up in two weeks if pain persists.
The doctor advises continuing swimming for back pain once the knee improves.
The doctor advises continuing the current medication regimen for depression and carpal tunnel.

case_description: joint pain; carpal tunnel release;chronic back pain;depression
